course.name: Claude Desktop Mastery
course.description: Master Claude Desktop with MCP servers, extensions, and integrations. Learn to build powerful AI-assisted workflows for web development. Final project: Connect your Claude Desktop to ClassroomNeo!

modules.title: Module 1: Introduction to Claude Desktop
modules.description: Understanding Claude Desktop's architecture and capabilities across different operating systems

steps.title: 1.1 What is Claude Desktop?
steps.content:
# What is Claude Desktop?

Claude Desktop is Anthropic's native application that brings Claude AI directly to your computer with powerful local capabilities.

## Why Claude Desktop?

- **Local File Access**: Read and work with files on your computer
- **MCP Protocol**: Connect to external tools and services
- **Extended Context**: Longer conversations with better memory
- **Privacy**: Some processing stays local
- **Integrations**: GitHub, databases, APIs, and more

## Desktop vs Web

| Feature | Claude.ai (Web) | Claude Desktop |
|---------|-----------------|----------------|
| File Access | Upload only | Full local access |
| MCP Servers | Limited | Full support |
| External Tools | No | Yes |
| Offline Work | No | Partial |

## What You'll Learn

By the end of this course, you'll be able to:
1. Configure Claude Desktop for your OS
2. Install and manage MCP servers
3. Connect to GitHub, Netlify, and databases
4. Build automated workflows
5. **Connect Claude Desktop to this classroom!**

steps.title: 1.2 Installation Guide (Choose Your OS)
steps.content:
# Installing Claude Desktop

## 🍎 MacOS Installation

1. **Download**: Visit [claude.ai/download](https://claude.ai/download)
2. **Install**: Open the `.dmg` file and drag to Applications
3. **First Launch**: Open from Applications folder
4. **Sign In**: Use your Anthropic account

### MacOS-Specific Features
- Native Apple Silicon support (M1/M2/M3)
- Spotlight integration
- Homebrew package management for MCP servers
- Terminal/zsh integration

## 🪟 Windows Installation

1. **Download**: Visit [claude.ai/download](https://claude.ai/download)
2. **Install**: Run the `.exe` installer
3. **First Launch**: Find in Start Menu
4. **Sign In**: Use your Anthropic account

### Windows-Specific Features
- PowerShell integration
- WSL2 support for Linux tools
- Windows Terminal compatibility
- Chocolatey/Scoop for package management

## ✅ Verification

After installation, verify by:
1. Opening Claude Desktop
2. Checking Settings → About for version
3. Testing a simple conversation

**Assignment**: Screenshot your Claude Desktop 'About' page and share!

steps.title: 1.3 Understanding the Claude Desktop Interface
steps.content:
# The Claude Desktop Interface

## Main Components

### 1. Conversation Panel
- Where you chat with Claude
- Supports markdown, code blocks, images
- Drag & drop files directly

### 2. Sidebar
- **Conversations**: Your chat history
- **Projects**: Organize related chats
- **Settings**: Configuration options

### 3. Input Area
- Text input with @ mentions
- File attachment button
- Voice input (if enabled)

### 4. Settings Menu

**Key Settings to Know:**
- **Model Selection**: Choose Claude version
- **MCP Servers**: Where the magic happens!
- **Appearance**: Light/Dark mode
- **Privacy**: Data handling preferences

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Action | MacOS | Windows |
|--------|-------|--------|
| New Chat | ⌘ + N | Ctrl + N |
| Settings | ⌘ + , | Ctrl + , |
| Search | ⌘ + K | Ctrl + K |
| Clear Chat | ⌘ + Shift + K | Ctrl + Shift + K |

## Pro Tips

1. **Use Projects** to organize by topic
2. **Pin important chats** for quick access
3. **Drag files** directly into the chat
4. **Use @ mentions** to reference MCP tools

modules.title: Module 2: MCP Servers - The Power Core
modules.description: Deep dive into Model Context Protocol (MCP) - the technology that makes Claude Desktop incredibly powerful

steps.title: 2.1 What is MCP?
steps.content:
# Model Context Protocol (MCP)

## The Game Changer

MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open protocol that allows Claude to:
- **Read** from external data sources
- **Write** to external services
- **Execute** commands and scripts
- **Connect** to APIs and databases

## How MCP Works

```
┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
│  Claude Desktop │────▶│   MCP Server    │────▶│ External Service│
│    (Client)     │◀────│   (Bridge)      │◀────│ (GitHub, DB...)│
└─────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘
```

## Types of MCP Servers

1. **File System** - Read/write local files
2. **Database** - Query PostgreSQL, SQLite, etc.
3. **API Connectors** - GitHub, Slack, etc.
4. **Custom Tools** - Build your own!

## Official vs Community Servers

**Official (by Anthropic):**
- Filesystem
- GitHub
- Slack
- Google Drive

**Community (Awesome-MCP):**
- Supabase
- Neon Database
- Netlify
- And hundreds more!

## Why This Matters

Without MCP: Claude can only chat
With MCP: Claude becomes your **AI-powered assistant** that can:
- Commit code to GitHub
- Deploy to Netlify
- Query your database
- Manage your files
- And much more!

steps.title: 2.2 MCP Configuration (MacOS)
steps.content:
# MCP Configuration for MacOS 🍎

## Configuration File Location

```bash
~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json
```

## Creating Your First Config

### Step 1: Open Terminal
```bash
# Create directory if needed
mkdir -p ~/Library/Application\ Support/Claude

# Create/edit config file
nano ~/Library/Application\ Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json
```

### Step 2: Basic Config Structure
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "filesystem": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": [
        "-y",
        "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem",
        "/Users/YOUR_USERNAME/Projects"
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

### Step 3: Prerequisites
```bash
# Install Node.js via Homebrew
brew install node

# Verify installation
node --version
npm --version
```

### Step 4: Restart Claude Desktop
- Quit Claude Desktop completely (⌘ + Q)
- Reopen from Applications

## Verify MCP is Working

Ask Claude:
> "Can you list the files in my Projects folder?"

If configured correctly, Claude will show your files!

## Troubleshooting MacOS

1. **Permission Issues**: Grant Full Disk Access in System Preferences
2. **Node not found**: Ensure Homebrew bin is in PATH
3. **Config syntax error**: Use `jsonlint` to validate

**Assignment**: Configure filesystem MCP and screenshot Claude listing your files!

steps.title: 2.3 MCP Configuration (Windows)
steps.content:
# MCP Configuration for Windows 🪟

## Configuration File Location

```
%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json
```

Or typically:
```
C:\Users\YOUR_USERNAME\AppData\Roaming\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json
```

## Creating Your First Config

### Step 1: Open PowerShell
```powershell
# Navigate to Claude config directory
cd $env:APPDATA\Claude

# Create directory if needed
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $env:APPDATA\Claude

# Create config file
notepad claude_desktop_config.json
```

### Step 2: Basic Config Structure
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "filesystem": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": [
        "-y",
        "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem",
        "C:\\Users\\YOUR_USERNAME\\Projects"
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

**⚠️ Windows Note**: Use double backslashes `\\` in paths!

### Step 3: Prerequisites
```powershell
# Install Node.js via Chocolatey
choco install nodejs

# Or via Scoop
scoop install nodejs

# Or download from nodejs.org

# Verify
node --version
npm --version
```

### Step 4: Restart Claude Desktop
- Close from System Tray (right-click → Exit)
- Reopen from Start Menu

## Verify MCP is Working

Ask Claude:
> "Can you list the files in my Projects folder?"

## Troubleshooting Windows

1. **Path Issues**: Use forward slashes `/` or escaped backslashes `\\`
2. **Node not found**: Run `refreshenv` or restart terminal
3. **UAC Blocks**: Run terminal as Administrator

**Assignment**: Configure filesystem MCP and screenshot Claude listing your files!

steps.title: 2.4 Managing Multiple MCP Servers
steps.content:
# Multi-Server MCP Configuration

## Adding Multiple Servers

Your config can have many servers running simultaneously:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "filesystem": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/path/to/projects"]
    },
    "github": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"],
      "env": {
        "GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN": "ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxx"
      }
    },
    "postgres": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-postgres"],
      "env": {
        "DATABASE_URL": "postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/db"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Environment Variables

Sensitive data goes in `env` block:

```json
"env": {
  "API_KEY": "your-secret-key",
  "DATABASE_URL": "connection-string"
}
```

## Server Categories

### 🔧 Development
- `filesystem` - File management
- `github` - Git operations
- `git` - Local git commands

### 🗄️ Databases
- `postgres` - PostgreSQL
- `sqlite` - SQLite
- `supabase` - Supabase integration

### 🚀 Deployment
- `netlify` - Deploy websites
- `vercel` - Vercel deployments

### 🔗 APIs
- `fetch` - HTTP requests
- `slack` - Team communication

## Checking Active Servers

In Claude Desktop:
1. Go to Settings
2. Look for MCP Servers section
3. Green = Active, Red = Error

## Performance Tip

Only enable servers you need! Each server uses resources.

modules.title: Module 3: GitHub Integration
modules.description: Connect Claude Desktop to GitHub for powerful code management and collaboration

steps.title: 3.1 Setting Up GitHub MCP Server
steps.content:
# GitHub MCP Integration

## Prerequisites
- GitHub account
- Personal Access Token (PAT)

## Creating a GitHub PAT

1. Go to GitHub → Settings → Developer Settings
2. Click 'Personal Access Tokens' → 'Tokens (classic)'
3. Generate new token with scopes:
   - `repo` (full control)
   - `read:org`
   - `gist`

**⚠️ SAVE THIS TOKEN! You won't see it again!**

## Configuration

### MacOS
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "github": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"],
      "env": {
        "GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN": "ghp_your_token_here"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Windows
Same config - just update path format if needed.

## What Claude Can Do with GitHub

✅ **Read Operations**
- List repositories
- View file contents
- Check issues and PRs
- Search code

✅ **Write Operations**
- Create repositories
- Push commits
- Create branches
- Open issues/PRs
- Merge PRs

## Test Your Setup

Ask Claude:
> "List my GitHub repositories"
> "Show me the README of my [repo-name] repository"

**Assignment**: Connect GitHub and ask Claude to list your repos!

steps.title: 3.2 Git Workflows with Claude
steps.content:
# Git Workflows Powered by Claude

## Common Workflows

### 1. Code Review
```
You: "Review the latest PR in my project-name repo"

Claude will:
- Fetch PR details
- Analyze code changes
- Provide suggestions
- Check for issues
```

### 2. Create Feature Branch
```
You: "Create a new branch called 'feature/user-auth' in my-app repo"

Claude will:
- Create the branch
- Confirm creation
- Suggest next steps
```

### 3. Commit Changes
```
You: "Commit the changes in /Projects/my-app with message 'Add login form'"

Claude will:
- Stage changes
- Create commit
- Push to remote
```

### 4. Issue Management
```
You: "Create an issue in my-repo: 'Bug: Login fails on Safari' with label 'bug'"
```

## Real-World Example

```
You: "I need to fix a bug in my website. Here's what's broken: [description]
      Can you:
      1. Create a bugfix branch
      2. Look at the relevant files
      3. Suggest a fix
      4. Commit and create a PR"
```

Claude handles the entire workflow!

## Pro Tips

1. **Be specific** about repo names
2. **Describe intent** not just actions
3. **Review before merge** - always verify Claude's changes

## Security Note

⚠️ Never share your PAT or config file
⚠️ Review all commits before pushing to production
⚠️ Use branch protection rules

**Assignment**: Have Claude create a branch, make a commit, and create a PR!
